title: Wellness and Health Services: Medical Treatment
description: Conroe ISD outlines how school staff respond when students need medical attention during the school day, including procedures for handling emergencies, administering first aid, and coordinating with parents or guardians. The policy also addresses when and how medications may be given to students at school, and what documentation is required to keep students safe. Parents and community members can rely on these guidelines to understand the care their children may receive if a health situation arises on campus.

timeline_local.summary: This update revises the nonprescription medication administration provision to expand and clarify the conditions under which an authorized employee may administer nonprescription medication to a student without further authorization or written protocol from the student's health care provider, specifying requirements related to written parental request, unexpired medication, original container, and label-consistent dosing.
changes_added: The authorized employee may administer nonprescription medication to a student without further authorization or written protocol from the student's health care provider if the district or school has received a written request to administer the medication from the parent, legal guardian, or other person having legal control of the student, the medication is unexpired and administered from a container that appears to be the original container and properly labeled, and the dose administered is consistent with the instructions on the container's label.
changes_removed: Nonprescription medication, upon a parent's written request, when properly labeled and in the original container.
timeline_local.notes: The cover page does not contain a standard action title heading (e.g., "Discuss Revisions to..." or "Adopt Revisions to..."); it only shows the policy name and metadata. Action has been set to 'unknown' as a result. The rationale is listed as 'Legislative change'. The redline on page 1 clearly shows red strikethrough (removed) and red underlined (added) text in item 2 of the Medication Provided By Parent section. All other policy text appears in black and is unchanged.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's FFAC policy expands who may authorize nonprescription medication administration to include legal guardians and other persons having legal control of the student (not just parents), and adds a new requirement that the dose administered must be consistent with the instructions on the container's label.
changes_added: legal guardian, or other person having legal control of the student giving their
and the dose administered is consistent with the instructions on the container's label
timeline_local.notes: The cover page does not contain a distinct board action title heading (e.g., "Discuss," "Adopt," etc.) — it appears to be the policy document itself rather than a separate recommendation/cover page. Action has been set to 'unknown' accordingly. The strikethrough removal is the possessive apostrophe-s ("'s") after "parent" which is replaced by the expanded phrase listing legal guardian and other persons with legal control. The red underlined additions expand the authorized requester and add the dosage consistency requirement.

description_local: This policy covers a set of updates to five school district rules governing employee legal protections, student grading and progress reporting, medical treatment at school, crisis intervention, and child abuse and neglect procedures — all revised to reflect recent changes in Texas law. These updates affect teachers and staff, students, and parents across the district. Parents and community members should be aware of these changes because they directly impact how schools communicate student progress, respond to health and safety emergencies, and handle situations involving potential harm to children.
